I have an existing Blazor Web App with Interactive Server (global interactivity).

I need a /privacy page that displays the privacy policy and reads a "consent"
cookie from the incoming HTTP request to show whether the user has already
accepted cookies.

The challenge is that the app uses global interactivity, but this page needs
to read HttpContext (for the cookie). HttpContext is only available during
static server-side rendering, not in interactive components. So this specific
page needs to opt out of interactive rendering and stay fully static, while
the rest of the app remains interactive.

Make sure App.razor is updated so that the routing knows to skip the interactive
render mode for this page.

            ? null
            : InteractiveServer;
}

data.content:
Done. Here's what was set up:

**`Privacy.razor`** — Decorated with `[ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]` so it renders as static SSR. This gives it access to `HttpContext`, where it reads the `"consent"` cookie to show whether the user has already accepted.

**`App.razor`** — Now computes `PageRenderMode` dynamically: if the matched endpoint has the `ExcludeFromInteractiveRoutingAttribute`, it passes `null` (static SSR); otherwise it passes `InteractiveServer`. The `<ReconnectModal>` is also conditionally rendered only for interactive pages.

**`Program.cs`** — Registers `IHttpContextAccessor` in DI.

All other pages remain globally interactive as before.
